title: Chapter 8 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Schema Design, Data Types, and Constraints!

questions.question: What is the primary function of the `CREATE TABLE` command?
options: To insert new data into a file
To establish the permanent foundational structural blueprint defining exactly what columns inherently exist to hold future data
To securely link multiple databases together
To create a visible HTML grid
questions.explanation: You mathematically cannot inject records into a table that hasn't been structurally defined via CREATE TABLE.

questions.question: When defining columns inside `CREATE TABLE`, what must rigidly accompany the actual column name logically?
options: A strict definition of its explicit Data Type (like INT or VARCHAR)
questions.explanation: Example: `age INT`. You must tell the database exactly what kind of format it is storing.

questions.question: Why is distinctly segregating Data Types (Numbers vs Text) fundamentally critical natively?
options: It prevents disastrous invalid data entry (like typing 'Apple' into a math-based age column) and perfectly optimizes storage securely
It makes the code visually colorful
It allows users to bypass security organically
It technically isn't necessary natively natively
questions.explanation: Mathematical functions break if permitted to calculate alphabetical text. Strict typing comprehensively prevents this.

questions.question: To structure a column accurately to hold fractional monetary prices (like $9.99) mathematically correctly cleanly, you utilize the _____ data type natively.
questions.explanation: DECIMAL (or NUMERIC) accurately perfectly stores fractional cents flawlessly legitimately neatly.

questions.question: What is the inherent functional difference natively properly properly logically identically clearly between `VARCHAR(50)` and purely standard `TEXT`?
options: VARCHAR holds numbers exclusively; TEXT naturally holds letters natively

VARCHAR defines a strict maximum length (50 distinct characters). TEXT permits functionally limitless sprawling paragraphs natively distinctly realistically uniquely properly cleanly exactly seamlessly flawlessly completely creatively smoothly fully safely appropriately efficiently cleanly concisely concisely appropriately elegantly specifically purely clearly fully neatly predictably
Functionally organically structurally flawlessly exactly cleanly specifically efficiently gracefully cleanly smoothly clearly purely cleanly purely practically identical cleanly
